name: Lu Meng
description: Lu Meng was a prominent general serving under the warlord Sun Quan during the late Eastern Han dynasty and Three Kingdoms period of China. Initially known for his lack of scholarly pursuits, Lu Meng famously heeded Sun Quan's advice and dedicated himself to learning, eventually becoming a well-read and strategic commander. This transformation is captured in the famous proverb, 'Lu Meng learns diligently.' His military prowess was instrumental in Sun Quan's expansion, particularly in the conquest of Jing Province from Liu Bei. Lu Meng orchestrated a brilliant campaign, feigning illness to lower Guan Yu's guard and then swiftly capturing key strategic points. He captured Guan Yu, who was later executed. Lu Meng's victory secured a vital territory for Wu, but he mysteriously died shortly after, with rumors attributing it to Guan Yu's vengeful spirit. Despite his relatively short career, Lu Meng is revered as a strategic genius and a testament to the power of self-improvement and lifelong learning. His life story is a popular subject in Chinese operas and folklore, often portraying him as a symbol of ambition, dedication, and the importance of intellectual development alongside martial skills. He represents the ideal of a warrior who combines strength with wisdom. His cunning strategies and eventual mastery of literature and military tactics cemented his place as one of the most influential figures of the Three Kingdoms period.
subtitle: The Diligent General of Wu

keyMyths.title: Lu Meng Learns Diligently
keyMyths.description: Sun Quan encouraged Lu Meng to study despite his initial aversion to it. Lu Meng dedicated himself to learning, becoming a respected scholar and strategist, proving that anyone can improve through effort. This story became a famous proverb.

keyMyths.title: Conquest of Jing Province
keyMyths.description: Lu Meng feigned illness to lure Guan Yu away from Jing Province, then launched a surprise attack, capturing the strategic territory for Wu. This decisive victory significantly shifted the balance of power during the Three Kingdoms period.

keyMyths.title: Lu Meng's Mysterious Death
keyMyths.description: Shortly after capturing Guan Yu, Lu Meng died suddenly, leading to rumors that Guan Yu's vengeful spirit haunted him. This event added a supernatural element to Lu Meng's story and highlighted the consequences of his actions.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: The primary historical source for Lu Meng's life and deeds.
sources.text: The primary historical source for Lu Meng's life and deeds.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A fictionalized account of the Three Kingdoms period that greatly popularized Lu Meng's story.
